item.title: "You have it in your power to make a difference"
item.dc:creator: unwaver

item.title: Laser printers, people.
item.description: tl;dr: Buy a laser printer. Unless you need to print tons of photos, buy a black-and-white laser printer. If you want colour, colour laser pritners aren't much more. Do NOT buy HP anything. Brother is, among others, a pretty decent brand. I did tech support for HP DeskJet printers back in the 90s. They were pretty good. But around 1996 is when it changed. HP all around went downhill, and now for 30 years they have been crap. No excuse for buying HP. It's crap. Buy a laser printer. I like being able to print in colour, so I bought a Brother colour laser 8 years ago. I don't print that often. I've replaced the black cartridge twice and some of the colours - still haven't replaced magenta. It's still trucking along perfectly and has worked with each version of Windows and Linux just fine. The ONLY reason to buy inkjet is if you print a TON of photographs. Ink is expensive and finicky - it dries and clogs. If you occasionally need photos printed in high quality, use a printing service. It's worth it - they're not expensive and it's much much cheaper than throwing away expensive ink at home.
item.dc:creator: daychilde

item.description:
1
2

18
19
20
21
22
23
24
25
26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @bucoda made this box a while back and I think it's badass!
27 Man Tax: Vladislav Tsarov
“The best moments in reading are when you come across something — a thought, a feeling, a way of looking at things — that you'd thought special, particular to you. And here it is, set down by someone else, a person you've never met, maybe even someone long dead. And it's as if a hand has come out, and taken yours.” - Alan Bennett, The History Boys I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting only on that, also comment about something you DID like. Balance the negativity. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We're also setting up Jack Box game night and movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work and pretty destitute. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, thank you so much.
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: They’re all good dogs, Brent
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.title: It was not an easy job but it's all I've ever known
item.description: Don't get me wrong, the world changes and you need to adapt, but this was faster than I had ever imagine. I work, or should say worked, in visual effects. For 15 years I spent anywhere from 40 to 90 hours a week giving my best to make movies look as good as possible. For a long time I thought despite the burnouts, insane hours, forever shrinking budgets, moving across the world to find work, I would make it work because I love what I do... But COVID arrived. First layoff because shootings stopped, 6 months surviving thanks to my partner. Then the Writer Guild strike followed by the Actor strike. Another 4 months surviving on my savings. Then the government shut down the tax incentives that made us competitive. Another 3 months getting by on what I saved since the last layoff. To help us out, a wave of "no VFX was used in the making of xxxx" started on movies with hundreds of heavy VFX shots, making our work invisible, with directors borderline ashame to put our names in the credit. And now AI trying to take over the entire industry. In 2019, I was going to work with hundreds of my peers, sharing our passion and ideas until late at night often 6 days a week. Today contracts are for a few weeks at best, cameras closed during meetings, I barely know the people I work with and there's usually not more than 8 to 10 of them. Once a show is delivered, another bunch replace them. Each show I see the same struggle in everyone's life. Some have to leave the country because their work visa expired. Some struggle to pay rent and are returning home, often in a different province, if not a different country. A lot of artist I worked with are now electrician, plumbers, IT, programmers, ... And with each of them leaving, it's decades of expertise gone forever. I'm starting university soon to become a psychologist myself, after realizing that what I loved the most in my job was the people, and they are now gone. I've held on to the hope that somehow, at some point, things might get better, but I've once again been laid off, and I don't know if this time it will be 3, 4, 6 months or just permanent. LinkedIn, the hell where 95% of the job market for my field happens, is dead silent. Just AI bros sharing about the latest AI model and more AI bros dreaming of how anyone can make a movie with prompts. It makes job hunting an even worse experience as I scroll through posts hoping to see a role and instead, keep seeing more and more people abandoning VFX and random tech bros redoing scenes from their favorite movies on any random AI generator. When a car maker closes a factory, it's thousands of people losing their jobs, journalists report on it. During the Hollywood strikes the media coverage was intense. The latest story of "Tilly Norwood" seems to be everywhere. All while thousands of VFX artists like me are silently left to disappear without much coverage. It's like we were never here. Sorry for the long post, I feel like I'm yelling into the void.
item.dc:creator: logkyles

item.title: Again, showing trump isn't the only trump in those doors
item.dc:creator: KatLuvr54

item.title: Judge Who Denied A DOJ Request For Voters Records, House Burned Down After An Explosion – SLED Is Investigating As Arson
item.description:
Liberal South Carolina Judge Diane Goodstein's home is burning to the ground after an explosion. SLED is investigating as arson and refer attack as Judge Goodstein had been receiving multiple threats. Her husband, a former State Senator was air-lifted to the hospital.
While Steven Miller rails about Judges.
SOUTH CAROLINA... "Judge Goodstein was walking on the beach when the fire started. Her husband, Arnie, was in the house with children and perhaps grandchildren. The family had to escape by jumping from a window or balcony..."

item.dc:creator: Docreport

item.title: I’ve been guilty of this
item.description: Most viral edit: Thanks for the upvotes, my dudes! I kinda didn’t expect this one to get out of usersub hence the lack of source or context. @keyblader1985 is correct, this post has nothing to do with Trump, or at least it didn’t when I posted it. I found the pic in some thread about Kristi Noem and the trend of Botox injections and big lips, they refer to it as Mar A Lago face. Kristi et al are assholes, and it’s easy for me to discard all my values and zero in on the low hanging fruit that is their appearance. Hence why this pic was a good reminder that for every asshole I make fun of in my rants or comments, I could be hurting others with real and uncontrollable issues. I was not aware of the seriousness of body dysmorphia, and I had some pretty misguided ideas on it, especially when it comes to men, here is a good summary on it if you want to learn more: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/9888-body-dysmorphic-disorder One new thing I learned from all the comments here that I will think about on my way to work. I made a post about the Derek guy who makes fun of right wing fashion choices. How does that fit into this updated framework? Original post: It’s a low hanging fruit and I’m sure I’m not the only one who does this. It’s a hard change to make but I’m trying.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars
